episodes.purpose: reproduce the failure and check the imports
episodes.what_it_did: ran the CLI and printed report.py's import header
episodes.outcome: report.py crashes immediately at 'import yaml' with ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'yaml'; it also imports requests and tabulate

episodes.purpose: survey which dependencies are already available vs missing
episodes.what_it_did: read requirements.txt and probed each dependency with pip list and import checks
episodes.outcome: requests (2.31.0) and tabulate (0.9.0) are already installed; PyYAML is the only missing dependency

episodes.purpose: install the one missing dependency and re-run
episodes.what_it_did: pip-installed pyyaml, confirmed the import, and re-ran the CLI
episodes.outcome: PyYAML 6.0.2 installed and importable; the import error is gone, but report.py now fails later with FileNotFoundError: data.yaml

episodes.what_it_did: listed the data files present, grepped the loader, and read report.py and data.json
episodes.outcome: data.yaml does not exist but data.json (same 'sales' shape) is present; report.py's load_data is hard-wired to read data.yaml via yaml.safe_load

episodes.purpose: bridge the data gap from what's available
episodes.what_it_did: converted the available data.json into data.yaml with PyYAML, then re-ran and spot-checked the output
episodes.outcome: data.yaml written from data.json; report.py now runs and prints the regional sales table (North/South/West with units and revenue)

episodes.purpose: verify the run and confirm report.py was left unchanged
episodes.what_it_did: re-ran the CLI and checked git status and the report.py diff
episodes.outcome: the report prints correctly; git shows only data.yaml added (untracked) and report.py's diff is empty, satisfying the 'do not change report.py' constraint
